title: Insomniac to Stay Involved With Miami’s Club Space & Factory Town Venues After Legal Settlement
summary: The global dance music company has "amicably resolved" its lengthy legal battle with a trio of Miami club operators.
body: Insomniac Events has reached a settlement that will keep the global dance music company involved in two Miami venues following a protracted legal battle with a group of the city’s club operators. In a joint statement to Billboard on Friday (July 10), reps for Insomniac and business partners David Sinopoli , Davide Danese and Jose Gabriel Coloma Cano say they “have amicably resolved their dispute.” Insomniac, partially owned by Live Nation, previously worked with the trio of men to run Miami’s Club Space and Factory Town venues until a legal fight broke out two years ago. Related Insomniac Sues Miami Club Operators for Trying to ‘Bully’ Their Way Into Control of Factory Town Venue DJ Quik Says His Son 'Made a Mistake' Following Murder Conviction, Pleads With Fans to Stop Asking About It Naoshi Fujikura of Universal Music Japan on Japan's Unique Superfan Culture & Global Ambitions: Billboard Global Power Players Interview “Insomniac will continue to operate Club Space alongside David Sinopoli and maintain its commitment to Factory Town,” the reps say of the settlement. “Davide Danese and Coloma Cano will continue to operate Jolene, along with Davi…

title: Big & Rich’s John Rich Sworn In as USDA Special Envoy for American Landowners: ‘Let’s Get to Work’
summary: The post was made official during his swearing in at the Oval Office.
body: Country star John Rich has been officially sworn in as Special Envoy for American Landowners, according to a White House social media post Friday (July 10). The post shows the Big & Rich member being sworn in surrounded by his family and shaking hands with President Donald Trump in the Oval Office. 🇺🇸 @JohnRich has officially been sworn in as Special Envoy for American Landowners in the Oval Office! pic.twitter.com/ZoZ284KX7D — The White House (@WhiteHouse) July 10, 2026 Earlier Friday, Rich also posted heralding the ceremony: “Today, I’ll be sworn in as ‘Special Envoy for American Landowners’ by President Trump at the Oval. My family will be there with me! (My Dad bought his first new suit in 25 years). I greatly thank @realDonaldTrump for giving me the opportunity to fight for my fellow Americans.” Today, I'll be sworn in as "Special Envoy For American Landowners" by President Trump at The Oval. My family will be there with me! (My Dad bought his first new suit in 25 years) I greatly thank @realDonaldTrump for giving me the opportunity to fight for my fellow Americans🇺🇸 — John Rich🇺🇸 (@johnrich) July 10, 2026 Rich also quote-tweeted the White …

title: Benny Blanco Offers First Taste of Spanish-Language Album ‘Hermoso’ With ‘Joven y Salvaje,’ Featuring Bb trickz
summary: The Spanish rapper speaks of the carelessness that comes with being young on the new song.
body: Benny Blanco is giving Spanish-language music a beautiful new meaning with his next project. The producer, who is set to release his forthcoming album Hermoso (Gorgeous) on Aug. 14, is letting fans take a look at what it’s all about. On Friday (June 11), the artist released “Joven y Salvaje” (young and wild), a song he produced to accompany the vocals of Spanish rapper Bb trickz. Related Benny Blanco Recalls His Mom Telling Him He Should Marry Selena Gomez Back in 2019: ‘My Mom Planted the Seed’ Selena Gomez Posts Sweet 38th Birthday Tribute to Husband Benny Blanco: ‘I Love You With All My Heart’ Benny Blanco’s Mom Had to Hide in Her Bathroom During Scary Home Invasion Its rhythmic foundation relies on a simple, repeating melodic structure, paired with a house-inspired beat. Within the track, Trickz delivers verses that capture the rebelliousness and occasional recklessness of youth. “Young and wild/ I haven’t slept, and it’s a Tuesday/ A stoley is in the garage/ I smoke zaza; I’m on Mars,” the rapper says in Spanish.  The music video of the song was also released on YouTube. Directed by Jake Schreier, the clip shows the artist — who pre…

title: Drake Makes $1 Million Bet on Conor McGregor to Win UFC Fight: ‘The Mac Is Back’
summary: McGregor returns to the Octagon on Saturday night to take on Max Holloway at UFC 329.
body: Return of the Mac. Drake has wagered a $1 million bet on Conor McGregor to win in his return to the ring this weekend at UFC 329. Drake posted his betting slip on Instagram on Friday (July 10) featuring a seven-figure wager on Stake with the belief that McGregor will defeat Max Holloway in McGregor’s comeback to the octagon in Las Vegas on Saturday night (July 11). Related Drake Makes Surprise Appearance on Die-Hard OVO Fan’s TikTok Live: ‘I Got a Lot of Love for You’ Drake Is Hosting Apology Parties for Anyone Named Janice in Several Cities: ‘Look Out for My Invitation’ Drake Pays Tribute to Tay Keith After Producer’s Death: ‘Endless and Eternal Gratitude’ Drake’s $1 million wager would net him $1.85 million for a total of $2.85 million if McGregor wins the welterweight bout. As of press time, Conor McGregor is an underdog in the fight with +180 odds, according to FanDuel. McGregor and Drake have been friends for a decade now after the UFC fighter revealed they connected in 2016 and met at one of Drizzy’s Summer Sixteen Tour stops later that year. “I’m a big fan of Drake. I had a meeting with Jimmy…

title: Hilary Duff Wears Her Own 2004 Throwback Concert Tee for 2 Sold-Out Nights at L.A.’s Forum
summary: The Lucky Me Tour continues Saturday night in Mountain View, California.
body: Hilary Duff has been bringing Y2K nostalgia to every night of her Lucky Me Tour since it kicked off last month, and across two sold-out nights at the L.A.-area Kia Forum in Inglewood, California, on Wednesday and Thursday nights, she turned back the calendar to 2004 with a very personal throwback T-shirt. Related Hilary Duff’s Lucky Me Tour Setlist: Every Song She Played on Night 1 in Florida Hilary Duff Explains Why It’s Better to Focus on Being ‘Yourself’ Than Worry About ‘Becoming More Impressive’ Watch Hilary Duff Sing 10-Year-Old Fan-Favorite Deep Cut for the First Time Ever Duff wore her very own 2004 concert tee for the occasion, which includes photos of the then-16-year-old pop star/actress on the front and the back and a full list of that year’s tour dates on the back. She also started selling a reprint of the tee at Forum merch booths and on her website . As seen in Billboard ‘s video from Thursday below, Duff wore the tee to perform her 2003 single “Why Not” from The Lizzie McGuire Movie soundtrack and her Metamorphosis album. Duff also re-recorded the song for her (Mine) EP this year as a well as a W…

title: Bad Bunny’s Ex-Girlfriend Can Keep Suing Him Over Voice Memo on ‘Un Verano Sin Ti’ Track, Court Rules
summary: Puerto Rico’s Supreme Court says Carliz De La Cruz Hernández can continue her suit over the line "Bad Bunny baby" on 2022's "Dos Mil 16", but not an earlier track.
body: The Supreme Court of Puerto Rico has ruled that Bad Bunny ’s ex-girlfriend can continue suing the star for allegedly featuring her voice saying the phrase “Bad Bunny baby” on the chart-topping album Un Verano Sin Ti without permission. In a Wednesday (July 8) ruling, obtained by Billboard and translated from Spanish by the service DeepL, a majority of justices on the island’s highest court upheld a series of previous rulings that sustained Carliz De La Cruz Hernández’s claims over the use of her voice on the 2022 song “Dos Mil 16”. However, the court said De La Cruz Hernández waited too long to sue over the same vocal sample on Bad Bunny’s 2015 track “Pa Ti,” and these claims are barred by the statute of limitations. The lawsuit will now move forward as to “Dos Mil 16” only. Related Bad Bunny’s Ex-Girlfriend Sues Him Over ‘Unauthorized’ Use of Her Voice on ‘Bad Bunny Baby’ Tagline DJ Quik Says His Son 'Made a Mistake' Following Murder Conviction, Pleads With Fans to Stop Asking About It Naoshi Fujikura of Universal Music Japan on Japan's Unique Superfan Culture & Global Ambitions: Billboard Global Power Players Interview De La …

title: ‘For You Fest’ Unveils Inaugural Lineup Featuring Sets From Your Favorite Podcasters, Influencers & Musicians
summary: Social media stars such as Trisha Paytas, Tana Mongeau, Drew Afualo and more are on the bill.
body: For You Fest is turning the feed you scroll through into a festival you can stroll through, announcing this week that its inaugural event will take place in November with programs led by social-media stars such as Quenlin Blackwell, Tana Mongeau, Trixie Mattel, Trisha Paytas, Drew Afualo, Caleb Hearon, Meg Stalter and more. Kicking off Saturday, Nov. 14, at Great Park in Irvine, Calif., the two-day festival will open with a live taping of Superfans , a new podcast hosted by Billboard executive editor Lyndsey Havens, featuring fans and a surprise guest. That’s one of several in-person recordings coming to the podcast stage, which itself is one of three main divisions of the event. There will also be a music stage as well as a fashion and beauty stage, plus dedicated areas for meet-and-greets and various workshops on the grounds that attendees can add on to their tickets. Blackwell and Owen Thiele are two more of the podcasters lined up for the first day of the festival, while Afualo and Hearon, Tana Mongeau, Stalter and more will bring their pods to the festival on day two, which is Nov. 15. Mattell, Paytas, Rebecca Black, Absolutely and others will perform on the music stage…

title: All of Future’s Albums Ranked From Worst to Best: Critics’ Picks
summary: How Pluto became an Atlanta trap architect.
body: Super Future, Pluto, Hendrix, FireMarshall Future, Future Pendergrass and The WIZRD are all Future personas and alter-egos that have fueled him as a trap architect and defining voice of rap in the 21st century. Future’s been as consistent as they come in hip-hop. When the Atlanta rap dignitary drops, fans know what to expect: hedonistic endeavors, toxic love, battles with drug addiction, opulent flexes in lavish locations and trap glory. “I don’t feel like nobody needs to be like me… exactly how I am. I only can do this,” Future told Billboard in 2022. “I’m the only one that can do this s–t, you know what I’m saying? That’s from my personal life to musically. I’m really one of none. I can live like this. People accept that, because it’s just me.” The 42-year-old returned on Friday (July 10) taking the mask off for his The Real Me album, which boasts zero features. Pluto set the bar high when he claimed that the “album of the century” was on the way with his 10th studio LP. Future’s set the standard in his own right as a mystifying commercial titan and prolific superstar with 11 No. 1s atop the Billboard 200 and a staggering 229 Billboard H…

title: Metallica’s ‘ReLoad’ Makes Top 10 Return After Remastered Reissue
summary: It’s No. 1 on Top Hard Rock Albums and top 10 on five more charts, including Top Album Sales.
body: Metallica’s ReLoad returns to Billboard ’s album charts after its remastered reissue, as the set, first released in 1997, hits the top 10 on six different charts (dated July 11), including No. 1 on Top Hard Rock Albums. (All versions of the album, old and new, are combined on the charts.) Here’s where ReLoad places on the latest Billboard album charts: Top Hard Rock Albums , No. 1 (debut) Top Rock Albums , No. 3 (debut) Top Rock & Alternative Albums , No. 4 (debut) Indie Store Album Sales , No. 7 (debut) Top Album Sales , No. 9 (reentry) Vinyl Albums , No. 7 (reentry) Billboard 200 , No. 16 (reentry) It’s worth noting that the four charts where ReLoad debuts this week did not exist when the album was originally released in 1997. Indie Store Album Sales launched in 2005, Top Rock Albums and Top Rock & Alternative Albums began in 2006, and Top Hard Rock Albums started in 2007. ReLoad debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard 200 in 1997 and marked the third chart-topper for the band. It reenters the Billboard 200 at No. 16, landing its first week on the chart since 1999. On Top Album Sales, ReLoad is amongst a busy top 10, where ATEEZ notches its seventh No. 1 with the debut of GOLD…

title: DoorDash Delivers With the ‘Summer of DashPass’
summary: DashPass members can get massive savings during the 2026 FIFA World Cup this summer.
body: All products and services featured are independently chosen by editors. However, Billboard may receive a commission on orders placed through its retail links, and the retailer may receive certain auditable data for accounting purposes. Calling all soccer fans! With 2026 FIFA World Cup in full swing, fans from all around the world are flocking to cities in the United States, Canada and Mexico to cheer for their favorite teams and to watch matches together in celebration of all things soccer. Throughout the international tournament, DoorDash, an Official Tournament Sponsor, is offering massive savings to make your World Cup watch-parties memorable and fun with the ordering and delivery company’s “ Summer of DashPass ” campaign. From right now until Wednesday, July 22, DashPass members can get exclusive deals at restaurants and retailers with savings up to 50% off orders on meals, groceries, tech and more. The deals, retailers and restaurants change up every week through the tournament, so sign up for DoorDash’s DashPass to get the most out of your World Cup summer and save big on popular brands, such as Pepsi, Burger King, Taco Bell, Best Buy, Cheesecake Fact…

title: Africa CDC and WHO launch joint continental Ebola response plan
summary: The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) and the World Health Organization (WHO) today launched a joint continental preparedness and response plan on the ongoing Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus. title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo
summary: How Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo didn't just dominate their era. They created one.

title: Unsafe food causes 866 million illnesses and 1.5 million deaths annually, young children at highest risk
summary: Children aged less than five years face almost three times the risk of illness from unsafe food than older children and adults, according to new estimates released today by the World Health Organization (WHO).

title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
summary: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

title: WHO urges governments to protect young people from addiction to tobacco and nicotine products
summary: Worldwide, at least 40 million children aged 13–15 use tobacco products, and young people’s use of e-cigarettes and nicotine pouches continues to rise. title: Experts convened by WHO advise on candidate treatments and vaccines for Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus
summary: In response to the current outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus occurring in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with cases also reported in Uganda, WHO convened several of its expert and advisory groups. title: WHO warns nicotine pouch brands targeting youth as sales surge
summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) warns about the rapid growth and aggressive marketing of nicotine pouches, especially targeting youth, amid limited regulation worldwide. title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
summary: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.
